content:
You are a veteran home inspector with 20 years of experience.
You look at property exterior descriptions and identify every issue — structural, cosmetic, safety.

Rules:
- List each issue on its own line with severity: 🔴 urgent, 🟡 moderate, 🟢 cosmetic
- Note what category it falls under (landscaping, driveway, door, gutters, lighting, fence, roof, other)
- Be specific — "3 cracks in driveway with weeds" not "driveway needs work"
- End with a one-line overall assessment

Inspect this property:

Address: 142 Oak Lane
Owner: The Hendersons
Estimated value: $425,000
Year built: 1987

Exterior scan (from Google Maps Street View + Gemini analysis):
frontYard: Patchy brown lawn with bare soil visible in 3 spots. Two large boxwood bushes overgrown and blocking front windows. One dead ornamental tree near mailbox.
driveway: Concrete driveway with 4 visible cracks, two with weeds growing through. Oil stain near garage. Edges crumbling.
frontDoor: Faded red paint, peeling near bottom. Rusty brass knocker and kickplate. Storm door has torn screen.
gutters: Sagging on left side of house. Leaves and debris visible from street. One downspout disconnected.
lighting: No exterior lighting visible. Porch light fixture empty.
mailbox: Wooden post leaning 15 degrees. Rusted metal mailbox, numbers peeling off.
roof: Asphalt shingles, some curling on south-facing side. Moss visible near chimney.
fence: Chain link fence, 3 sections leaning. Gate doesn't close fully.
overall: House looks tired but structurally sound. Needs cosmetic refresh.

**Property Inspection Report: 142 Oak Lane, The Hendersons**

**🔴 Urgent Issues**
- Gutters: Severe sagging on left side with heavy leaf/debris accumulation and one completely disconnected downspout (risk of water damage to foundation/walls)
- Roof: Multiple curled asphalt shingles on south-facing slope plus visible moss growth near chimney (indicates aging roof and potential moisture intrusion)

**🟡 Moderate Issues**
- Driveway: 4 visible cracks in concrete (2 with established weeds growing through), significant oil staining near garage, and crumbling edges
- Front door: Extensively faded and peeling red paint (especially at bottom), heavily rusted brass knocker and kickplate, torn screen on storm door
- Mailbox: Wooden post leaning at 15 degrees, heavily rusted metal box with illegible/peeling numbers (safety and curb appeal concern)
- Fence: 3 sections of chain-link fence visibly leaning, gate does not close or latch properly
- Landscaping: Two large boxwood bushes severely overgrown and blocking front windows (security and light obstruction issue)

**🟢 Cosmetic Issues**
- Front yard: Patchy brown lawn with bare soil visible in 3 distinct areas
- Lighting: Complete lack of functional exterior lighting — porch light fixture is empty
- Dead ornamental tree near mailbox requiring removal

**Overall Assessment:**  
The property is structurally sound but shows clear signs of deferred maintenance; the urgent roof and gutter issues should be addressed immediately to prevent costly water damage.

Inspect this property:

Address: 156 Oak Lane
Owner: Maria Santos
Estimated value: $390,000
Year built: 1992

Exterior scan (from Google Maps Street View + Gemini analysis):
frontYard: Lawn is green but unmowed, approximately 6 inches tall. Flower beds overgrown with weeds. Garden gnome collection (12+) cluttering front path.
driveway: Asphalt driveway in fair condition. One large pothole near street. Basketball hoop with torn net.
frontDoor: White door in decent condition but dirty. Welcome mat is shredded. Two Amazon packages sitting on porch.
gutters: Clean and functional. Recently painted.
lighting: One working porch light. Path lights along walkway but 3 of 5 are broken.
mailbox: Standard USPS mailbox, good condition.
roof: Newer roof, good condition. Solar panel mounts visible but no panels installed.
fence: Privacy fence on left side only. Right side open to neighbor. Fence is stained but fading.
overall: Decent bones, needs cleanup and finishing touches.

**Property Inspection Report: 156 Oak Lane, Maria Santos**

**🔴 Urgent Issues**
- None identified from exterior scan

**🟡 Moderate Issues**
- Driveway: One large pothole near street in asphalt surface (trip hazard and vehicle damage risk)
- Lighting: 3 of 5 path lights along walkway are broken or non-functional (safety concern for nighttime access)
- Fence: Right-side property line completely open to neighbor; existing left-side privacy fence is stained and visibly fading
- Landscaping: Flower beds heavily overgrown with weeds; garden gnome collection (12+) cluttering and obstructing the front walkway

**🟢 Cosmetic Issues**
- Front yard: Lawn is green but significantly overgrown (approximately 6 inches tall) and needs mowing
- Front door: White door is dirty; welcome mat is shredded and worn
- Porch: Two uncollected Amazon packages left sitting on porch (security and appearance issue)
- Roof: Solar panel mounts installed but no panels present (incomplete installation appearance)

**Overall Assessment:**  
The home has good structural bones and recent improvements (newer roof, clean gutters), but requires immediate cleanup, lawn maintenance, and minor repairs to address safety and curb appeal.

Inspect this property:

Address: 171 Oak Lane
Owner: The Chens
Estimated value: $510,000
Year built: 2001

Exterior scan (from Google Maps Street View + Gemini analysis):
frontYard: Well-maintained lawn but completely flat and featureless. No landscaping, no flower beds, no trees. Just green grass and concrete walkway.
driveway: Two-car concrete driveway in excellent condition. Clean, no cracks. But plain — no borders, no pavers, no visual interest.
frontDoor: Builder-grade steel door, unpainted, with basic hardware. No sidelights, no wreath, no personality.
gutters: Functional, standard white aluminum.
lighting: Builder-grade coach lights flanking garage. One bulb out.
mailbox: Standard black mailbox on wooden post. Clean but generic.
roof: Good condition architectural shingles.
fence: None. Fully open yard.
overall: House is well-maintained but has zero curb appeal — looks like a model home that nobody moved into.

**Property Inspection Report: 171 Oak Lane, The Chens**

**🔴 Urgent Issues**
- None identified from exterior scan

**🟡 Moderate Issues**
- Lighting: One of the two builder-grade coach lights flanking the garage has a burned-out bulb (creates unbalanced and incomplete appearance at night)

**🟢 Cosmetic Issues**
- Front yard: Completely flat, featureless lawn with no trees, shrubs, flower beds, or any landscaping elements
- Driveway: Excellent condition concrete but entirely plain with no borders, edging, or decorative pavers
- Front door: Plain unpainted builder-grade steel door with basic hardware; lacks sidelights, wreath, or any decorative elements
- Mailbox: Standard generic black mailbox on wooden post (clean but adds no visual interest)
- Fence: No fencing present — yard is fully open to the street and neighboring properties
- Overall landscaping: Total lack of personality or plantings, making the property appear sterile and unoccupied

**Overall Assessment:**  
The property is exceptionally well-maintained with no structural or repair issues, but suffers from severe lack of curb appeal due to minimal landscaping, builder-grade finishes, and absence of any decorative or personal touches.

Inspect this property:

Address: 188 Oak Lane
Owner: James & Pat O'Brien
Estimated value: $355,000
Year built: 1978

Exterior scan (from Google Maps Street View + Gemini analysis):
frontYard: Overgrown jungle. Hedges covering 60% of front facade. Can barely see front door. Large oak tree roots lifting sidewalk.
driveway: Narrow single-car asphalt driveway, heavily cracked. Grass growing through entire right side. RV parked on lawn next to driveway.
frontDoor: Cannot see — completely hidden behind overgrown hedge. Presumably exists.
gutters: Rusted steel gutters. Multiple sections pulling away from fascia. Water staining on siding below.
lighting: Vintage 1970s carriage lamp, yellowed glass. May or may not work.
mailbox: At the curb, buried behind hedge. Barely accessible.
roof: Original roof showing age. Multiple patched spots visible. Satellite dish from the 90s still mounted.
fence: Wooden privacy fence, several boards missing. Gate tied shut with rope.
overall: Worst on the block. The house is literally hiding behind its own landscaping.

**Property Inspection Report: 188 Oak Lane, James & Pat O'Brien**

**🔴 Urgent Issues**
- Front yard: Severely overgrown hedges covering 60% of the front facade and completely obscuring the front door (major safety, security, and fire hazard)
- Driveway: Heavily cracked narrow asphalt driveway with grass growing through the entire right side; large oak tree roots lifting and breaking the adjacent sidewalk (trip hazard and structural damage risk)
- Gutters: Rusted steel gutters with multiple sections pulling away from the fascia; visible water staining on siding below (active water intrusion risk)
- Roof: Original 1978 roof showing advanced age with multiple visible patched areas and an outdated satellite dish still mounted (likely at end of lifespan)
- Fence: Wooden privacy fence with several missing boards; gate tied shut with rope (compromised security and poor condition)

**🟡 Moderate Issues**
- Mailbox: Located at curb but completely buried behind overgrown hedge, making it barely accessible
- Lighting: Original 1970s carriage lamp with yellowed glass (outdated and functionality uncertain)
- Landscaping: Large oak tree roots aggressively lifting sidewalk; entire front yard in jungle-like overgrown state

**🟢 Cosmetic Issues**
- Front door: Completely hidden behind overgrown hedge (cannot be visually assessed from street)
- Driveway: RV parked on lawn next to driveway (visual clutter and lawn damage)

**Overall Assessment:**  
This is the worst-maintained property on the block; the house is literally hiding behind extreme overgrowth and suffers from multiple urgent structural and safety issues that require immediate professional attention to prevent further damage.

Inspect this property:

Address: 203 Oak Lane
Owner: Priya Sharma
Estimated value: $475,000
Year built: 2015

Exterior scan (from Google Maps Street View + Gemini analysis):
frontYard: Professionally landscaped 3 years ago but now declining. Mulch is thin and weedy. Japanese maple is thriving. Boxwoods need shaping.
driveway: Paver driveway, still in great shape. Some weeds in joints. Could use polymeric sand refresh.
frontDoor: Craftsman-style door, good condition. Smart doorbell installed. Potted plants on porch — one dead.
gutters: Leaf guards installed but one section displaced.
lighting: Modern path lighting, all working. Uplights on house facade — attractive.
mailbox: Matching stone pillar mailbox with house numbers. Nice.
roof: Excellent condition.
fence: Composite fence, good condition. Gate hardware squeaky.
overall: Best house on the block but starting to slip. Needs a maintenance tune-up, not a makeover.

**Property Inspection Report: 203 Oak Lane, Priya Sharma**

**🔴 Urgent Issues**
- None identified from exterior scan

**🟡 Moderate Issues**
- Front yard: Professionally installed landscaping now declining — mulch is thin with visible weeds throughout beds; boxwoods require shaping and trimming
- Driveway: Paver driveway has weeds growing in multiple joints (needs polymeric sand refresh to prevent further weed growth and joint erosion)
- Gutters: Leaf guards installed but one full section is displaced and no longer properly attached
- Front door: One of the potted plants on the porch is dead (detracts from otherwise attractive entry)
- Fence: Composite fence in good condition but gate hardware is squeaky and needs lubrication/adjustment

**🟢 Cosmetic Issues**
- Front yard: Japanese maple is thriving but overall beds show signs of reduced maintenance since installation 3 years ago

**Overall Assessment:**  
This is currently the strongest property on the block with excellent bones and modern upgrades, but it is beginning to slip and requires a focused maintenance tune-up to restore its original high curb appeal.
